topic: Solar PV LCOE benchmarks for Brazil
summary: Brazil has some of the lowest solar PV costs globally. Utility-scale solar LCOE ranges from $25-45/MWh in high-irradiance regions. Distributed generation costs are higher at $60-90/MWh but declining rapidly.

findings.fact: Brazil added 12.8 GW of solar capacity in 2023, reaching 37 GW total installed
Porto Alegre region GHI: 1,500-1,700 kWh/m2/year (moderate-high for Brazil)
Brazil net metering (MMGD) program allows distributed generation up to 5MW with grid credit
findings.note: Policy under review — net metering rules may change

relevance: Informs CAPEX/LCOE assumptions in technology catalog and financing feasibility for Porto Alegre solar projects
